# Analysis — Game Theory #20: Mid-Term Examination

- **video_id:** `ue8y5e3HnHE`
- **canonical_url:** https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ue8y5e3HnHE
- **series / episode:** Predictive History · Game Theory #20

- **upload_date (YouTube):** 20260408 (confirm)
- **analyzed_at:** 2026-04-09 (ingest stub)

## Thesis / aim

Lecture opens as a **midterm examination**: audience-submitted questions (including categorized Twitter batch) on how prior Game Theory themes apply to the **US–Iran war window**, then broad **metaphysical-institutional** mapping (eschatology, agents, China as system extension), **Trump** as strategic chaos, **multipolar empire** sketches, and **individual ethics** (family, community, doubt). The Iran segment treats the announced **ceasefire** and **10-point plan** frame as **non-credible** and ties battlefield persistence to **Mosaic** decentralization and **proxy** interpretation gaps (Lebanon / Hezbollah).

## Next step
